cases.question: What is the average, highest, and total number of transactions for each transaction status (pending, completed, etc.)?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)-[:SENT]->(t:Transaction)
WITH t.status AS status, a, count(t) AS txnCountPerAccount
RETURN status,
       sum(txnCountPerAccount)              AS totalTransactions,
       round(avg(txnCountPerAccount), 2)   AS avgTransactionsPerAccount,
       max(txnCountPerAccount)             AS highestTransactionsPerAccount
ORDER BY status

cases.question: Which accounts are indirectly linked to each other through chains of shared personal details? I want to see account pairs that are connected through two to four intermediate links.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{2,4}-(b:Account)
WHERE elementId(a) < elementId(b)
WITH a, b, count(*) AS pathCount
RETURN a.accountID     AS accountA,
       a.accountNumber AS accountNumberA,
       b.accountID     AS accountB,
       b.accountNumber AS accountNumberB,
       pathCount
ORDER BY pathCount DESC
LIMIT 100

cases.question: What is the shortest chain of linked accounts connecting a savings account to a checking account?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH p = SHORTEST 1
  (start:Account {accountType: 'Savings'})
  -[:TRANSACTED_TO|SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{1,}
  (end:Account {accountType: 'Checking'})
WHERE start <> end
RETURN
  start.accountID        AS savingsAccount,
  end.accountID          AS checkingAccount,
  length(p)              AS hops,
  [n IN nodes(p) | n.accountID + ' (' + n.accountType + ')'] AS chain

cases.question: Which customers sit at the center of the most financial connections in our network? For each, show whether they have any open alerts or investigations.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c:Customer)
WITH c ORDER BY c.betweenness DESC LIMIT 10
MATCH (c)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a:Account)
OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:HAS_ALERT]->(alert:Alert)-[:HAS_STATUS]->(as:AlertStatus)
WHERE as.status IN ['open', 'investigating']
RETURN
  c.name                          AS customer,
  c.customerID                    AS customerID,
  round(c.betweenness, 4)         AS betweenness,
  c.fraudFlag                     AS fraudFlag,
  count(DISTINCT alert)           AS activeAlertCount,
  collect(DISTINCT as.status)     AS activeAlertStatuses
ORDER BY betweenness DESC

cases.question: Which transactions are unusually large compared to other transactions of the same type? Show transactions that exceed the average for their category.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)
WITH t.type AS txType, avg(t.amount) AS avgAmount
MATCH (tx:Transaction {type: txType})
WHERE tx.amount > avgAmount
RETURN tx.transactionID,
       tx.type,
       tx.amount,
       round(avgAmount, 2)          AS typeAvgAmount,
       round((tx.amount - avgAmount) / avgAmount * 100, 2) AS pctAboveAvg,
       tx.status,
       tx.date
ORDER BY tx.type, pctAboveAvg DESC

       round(c.pagerank, 4)  AS pagerank,
       round(c.betweenness, 4) AS betweenness,

cases.question: Find pairs of accounts that share personal details where at least one account belongs to a customer flagged for fraud. Who owns these accounts?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a1:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-(a2:Account)
WHERE elementId(a1) < elementId(a2)
MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a1)
MATCH (c2:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a2)
WHERE c1.fraudFlag = 'True' OR c2.fraudFlag = 'True'
RETURN
  a1.accountID        AS account1ID,
  a1.accountNumber    AS account1Number,
  c1.name             AS owner1,
  c1.fraudFlag        AS owner1Flagged,
  a2.accountID        AS account2ID,
  a2.accountNumber    AS account2Number,
  c2.name             AS owner2,
  c2.fraudFlag        AS owner2Flagged
ORDER BY owner1Flagged DESC, owner2Flagged DESC

cases.question: Which accounts received unusually large sums of money within a single month in 2025? Flag any account that received more than $10,000 in one calendar month.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)-[:RECEIVED]->(a:Account)
WHERE t.date.year = 2025
  AND t.status = 'Completed'
WITH a,
     t.date.year  AS year,
     t.date.month AS month,
     sum(t.amount) AS totalReceived
WHERE totalReceived > 10000
RETURN a.accountID,
       a.accountNumber,
       a.accountType,
       a.status,
       year,
       month,
       round(totalReceived, 2) AS totalReceived
ORDER BY totalReceived DESC

cases.question: Are there customers in the same fraud network cluster who are also sharing phone numbers or email addresses with each other? This could indicate coordinated activity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:USES_PHONE|USES_EMAIL]->(shared)<-[:USES_PHONE|USES_EMAIL]-(c2:Customer)
WHERE c1.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
  AND c1.louvainCommunity = c2.louvainCommunity
  AND c1.customerID < c2.customerID
WITH c1, c2,
     collect(DISTINCT
       WHEN 'PhoneNumber' IN labels(shared) THEN 'phone:' + shared.phoneNumber
            'Email'       IN labels(shared) THEN 'email:' + shared.email
       ELSE 'unknown' END
     ) AS sharedIdentifiers
RETURN
  c1.louvainCommunity          AS fraudCluster,
  c1.name                      AS customer1,
  c1.fraudFlag                 AS c1FraudFlag,
  c2.name                      AS customer2,
  c2.fraudFlag                 AS c2FraudFlag,
  sharedIdentifiers,
  size(sharedIdentifiers)      AS sharedCount
ORDER BY fraudCluster, sharedCount DESC

cases.question: Among the most influential accounts in our network, which pairs are most directly connected through shared identity links? Show the tightest connections first.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]->(b:Account)
WITH a, b, (a.pagerank + b.pagerank) AS combinedInfluence
ORDER BY combinedInfluence DESC
LIMIT 20
RETURN
  a.accountID          AS account1ID,
  a.accountNumber      AS account1Number,
  round(a.pagerank, 4) AS account1PageRank,
  b.accountID          AS account2ID,
  b.accountNumber      AS account2Number,
  round(b.pagerank, 4) AS account2PageRank,
  round(combinedInfluence, 4) AS combinedInfluence

cases.question: Are there active accounts sending money through multiple intermediate accounts before reaching a destination — a pattern that could indicate layering in money laundering? Show account pairs with multiple such routing paths.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH path = (src:Account {status: 'Active'})-[:TRANSACTED_TO]->{2,4}(dst:Account {status: 'Active'})
WHERE src <> dst
  AND ALL(n IN nodes(path)[1..-1] WHERE n.status = 'Active')
WITH src, dst, collect(path) AS paths
WHERE size(paths) >= 2
RETURN
  src.accountID        AS sourceAccount,
  src.accountNumber    AS sourceAccountNumber,
  dst.accountID        AS destinationAccount,
  dst.accountNumber    AS destinationAccountNumber,
  size(paths)          AS routingPathCount,
  [p IN paths | [n IN nodes(p) | n.accountID]] AS routingPaths
ORDER BY routingPathCount DESC
LIMIT 20

gate_details.reason: Syntax error: {neo4j_code: Neo.ClientError.Security.Unauthorized} {message: The client is unauthorized due to authentication failure.} {gql_status: 42NFF} {gql_status_description: error: syntax error or access rule violation - permission/access denied. cases.question: Mark all customers who belong to the same network cluster as known fraudsters as 'suspicious due to network association'. Process them in small batches to avoid overloading the system.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (fraudster:Customer {fraudFlag: 'True'})
WHERE fraudster.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
WITH collect(DISTINCT fraudster.louvainCommunity) AS fraudCommunities

MATCH (c:Customer)
WHERE c.louvainCommunity IN fraudCommunities
  AND c.fraudFlag <> 'True'
  AND c.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL

CALL (c) {
  SET c.fraudFlag = 'suspicious due to network association'
} IN TRANSACTIONS OF 500 ROWS ON ERROR CONTINUE

RETURN count(*) AS markedSuspicious

cases.question: Search for customers with the surname 'Poole' and trace how many accounts in our system are linked to their accounts — directly or through up to three degrees of shared identity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('customerNames', 'Poole') YIELD node AS customer, score
WHERE customer.name ENDS WITH 'Poole'
MATCH (customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(seed:Account)
WITH collect(DISTINCT customer.name) AS poole_customers,
     collect(DISTINCT seed)          AS seedAccounts
UNWIND seedAccounts AS seed
MATCH (seed)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{1,3}(linked:Account)
WHERE NOT linked IN seedAccounts
WITH poole_customers, seedAccounts, collect(DISTINCT linked) AS linkedAccounts
RETURN
  poole_customers,
  size(seedAccounts)   AS poole_account_count,
  size(linkedAccounts) AS linked_account_count,
  [a IN linkedAccounts | a.accountID] AS linked_account_ids
